env.title: Environment Variables
env.description: Next.js has built-in support for environment variables via .env files. Learn how to safely use them on the server and client.
env.envHierarchyDesc: Next.js loads environment variables from multiple files in a specific order. Later files override earlier ones.
env.serverOnlyDesc: By default, environment variables are only available on the server. They are never sent to the browser.
env.clientSideDesc: To expose a variable to the browser, prefix it with NEXT_PUBLIC_. Next.js inlines these values at build time.
env.nodeEnvText: This value is read from codeprocess.env.NODE_ENV at render time on the server:
env.nodeEnvNote: NODE_ENV is automatically set by Next.js: "development" when running codenext dev, "production" when running codenext build && next start.
env.neverExposeText: Never prefix sensitive values like API keys, database URLs, or tokens with codeNEXT_PUBLIC_. They will be embedded in your JavaScript bundle and visible to anyone.
env.gitIgnoreText: Always add code.env.local to your code.gitignore. Commit code.env with safe defaults and use code.env.local for real secrets during development.
env.buildTimeText: codeNEXT_PUBLIC_ values are replaced at build time, not runtime. Changing them requires a rebuild to take effect.
env.useServerComponentsText: Prefer reading secrets in Server Components or API routes. This keeps them entirely off the client and out of the JavaScript bundle.

redirects.title: Redirects & Rewrites
redirects.description: Configure URL redirects and rewrites in next.config.ts to control routing without writing application code.
redirects.redirectsDesc: Redirects send the user to a different URL. They return a 307 (temporary) or 308 (permanent) status code.
redirects.permanentText: Use codepermanent: true when a URL has moved forever. Browsers and search engines cache this redirect. The old URL transfers its SEO value to the new one.
redirects.temporaryText: Use codepermanent: false for temporary redirects. Browsers do not cache this, so the original URL remains the canonical source.
redirects.rewritesDesc: Rewrites map a URL to a different destination without changing the browser's address bar. The user sees the original URL.
redirects.beforeFilesText: strongbeforeFiles — Runs before any page or static file is matched. Useful for proxying API calls to an external backend.
redirects.afterFilesText: strongafterFiles — Runs after pages are checked but before public files. Useful for internal URL aliases.
redirects.fallbackText: strongfallback — Runs last, only if no page or file matched. Useful for proxying to a legacy app.
redirects.patternMatchingDesc: Both redirects and rewrites support dynamic segments and wildcards.
redirects.domainMigrationText: Redirect all old URLs to new ones with codepermanent: true to preserve SEO when restructuring your site.
redirects.apiProxyText: Use codebeforeFiles rewrites to proxy code/api/* to an external API server, avoiding CORS issues entirely.
redirects.vanityUrlsText: Rewrite code/pricing to code/marketing/pricing-page for clean public URLs while keeping your internal structure organized.
redirects.incrementalMigrationText: Use codefallback rewrites to send unmatched routes to a legacy application while migrating pages one by one.

headers.title: Headers & CSP
headers.description: Add custom HTTP headers in next.config.ts to improve security, caching, and compliance. Content Security Policy (CSP) helps prevent XSS attacks.
headers.customHeadersDesc: Define headers that Next.js will add to responses matching specific paths.
headers.securityHeadersDesc: These headers protect against common web vulnerabilities. Add them to every production site.
headers.referrerPurpose: Controls referrer info sent with requests
headers.hstsPurpose: Forces HTTPS for all future visits
headers.cspDesc: CSP tells the browser which sources of content are allowed. It is the most effective defense against XSS attacks.
headers.defaultSrcText: Fallback for all resource types not explicitly listed. Setting it to code'self' restricts loading to your own domain only.
headers.scriptSrcText: Controls which scripts can execute. Use nonces or hashes instead of code'unsafe-inline' in production for maximum security.
headers.styleSrcText: Controls which stylesheets are allowed. Many CSS-in-JS libraries require code'unsafe-inline', which weakens this directive.
headers.frameAncestorsText: Modern replacement for X-Frame-Options. Setting it to code'none' prevents your site from being loaded in any iframe.
headers.whyNoncesText: A nonce is a random value generated per request. Only scripts with a matching codenonce attribute will execute. This eliminates the need for code'unsafe-inline' and provides strong XSS protection, since attackers cannot guess the nonce.

bundle.title: Bundle Analyzer
bundle.description: Visualize your JavaScript bundle to find and eliminate bloat. The bundle analyzer generates an interactive treemap of every module in your build.
bundle.setupDesc: Install @next/bundle-analyzer and configure next.config.ts to enable it with an environment variable.
bundle.runningDesc: Set the ANALYZE environment variable before building to generate the report.
bundle.largeRectanglesText: strongLarge rectangles — Bigger boxes mean bigger modules. Look for unexpectedly large dependencies.
bundle.duplicateModulesText: strongDuplicate modules — The same library appearing multiple times may indicate version conflicts.
bundle.unusedLibrariesText: strongUnused libraries — Libraries you imported but barely use still contribute their full size to the bundle.
bundle.treeShakingDesc: Tree shaking removes unused exports from your bundle. It works automatically with ES modules but can fail silently.
bundle.goodImportLabel: Good — Imports Only What You Need
bundle.dynamicImportsDesc: Load heavy components only when they are needed using next/dynamic or React.lazy.
bundle.auditDepsText: Run the analyzer regularly. Remove unused packages and replace heavy ones with lighter alternatives (e.g., codedate-fns instead of codemoment).
bundle.useServerComponentsText: Server Components send zero JavaScript to the client. Keep data fetching and heavy logic in Server Components and only use code"use client" for interactivity.
bundle.lazyLoadText: Use codenext/dynamic for components not visible on initial load: modals, tabs, charts, and anything behind user interaction.
bundle.checkPackageSizeText: Use bundlephobia.com to check the size cost of any npm package before adding it to your project.
